City Council
Regular Meeting
1/12/2026 5:30 PM
Sullivan Chamber
Cambridge City Hall   795 Massachusetts Ave   Cambridge, MA 02139
Downloads:
Agenda Summary Agenda Packet
Call to Order
Roll Call
Pledge of Allegiance
Public Comment
I. City Manager's Agenda
1. CMA 2026 #1 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to a federal update including an update on relevant court cases.
City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
a. 2026-01-12 City Council Federal Litigation Tracker
2. CMA 2026 #2 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the appointment of the following members to the Police Review and Advisory Board.
City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
a. Caleb Zachary Demers Police Review and Advisory Board Appointment 2a
b. Belicia Wilkinson Police Review and Advisory Board Appointment 2b
3. CMA 2026 #3 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the appointment of the following members to the Mid Cambridge Neighborhood Conservation District Commission.
City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
a. Aaron Canto Mid Cambridge NCD Appointment 3a
b. Alex Van Praagh Mid Cambridge NCD Appointment 3d
c. Catherine Tice Mid Cambridge NCD Appointment 3i
d. Felix Rosen Mid Cambridge NCD Appointment 3f
e. Jacquelyn Fahey Sandell Mid Cambridge NCD Appointment 3g
f. Katinka Hakuta Mid Cambridge NCD Appointment 3h
g. Ksenia Kaladiouk Mid Cambridge NCD Appointment 3c
h. Min Ho Kim Mid Cambridge NCD Appointment 3e
i. Pauke Corstens Mid Cambridge NCD Appointment 3b
j. Sara Gardner Mid Cambridge NCD Appointment 3j
4. CMA 2026 #4 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the Final Landmark Designation Report for the Nathaniel Stickney House at 45 Mt. Auburn Street.
City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
a. 45 Mt Auburn St Final Landmark Report
b. 45 Mt. Auburn Transmittal letter
c. 45 Mt. Auburn Order
5. CMA 2026 #5 : Transmitting Communication from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the appropriation of $100,000, received from the Mass Broadband Institute to the Grant Fund Library Other Ordinary Maintenance account. Funds will be used to help support local digital navigators and provide devices to residents in need, including the distribution of up to 190 laptops and hotspots to qualifying residents in need; and supporting the work of digital navigators serving individuals at the Cambridge Public Library and Cambridge Public Schools.
City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
II. Policy Order and Resolution List
1. POR 2026 #1 : That the City Manager is requested to direct the appropriate City departments, including the Department of Transportation, to evaluate and install a series of speed humps along Fayerweather Street, with particular attention to the stretch between Walnut Street and Field Street.
Policy Order Printout
2. POR 2026 #2 : That the City Manager is requested to direct the City Solicitor and the Community Development Department to review and report back to the Council on (i) legal parameters related to City funding and Tourism Destination Marketing District restrictions, and (ii) oversight and accountability related to the operation of the visitor information kiosk, and related governance expectations, including alignment with City priorities.
Policy Order Printout
3. POR 2026 #3 : That the City Manager is requested to conduct a comprehensive review of existing safety and security measures at Cambridge City Hall, which shall include an assessment of physical access controls, visitor screening practices, on-site security staffing and training, emergency response and evacuation protocols, and the use of security technologies such as surveillance systems and alarm monitoring.
Policy Order Printout
4. POR 2026 #4 : That the City Council go on record acknowledging 2026 as the Centennial Celebration of Negro History Week, and recognizing its enduring contribution to the nation’s understanding of itself.
Policy Order Printout
5. POR 2026 #5 : That the City Manager is requested to work with relevant departments to prepare for a Council discussion on best future uses for City-owned properties and the processes for redevelopment.
Policy Order Printout
6. POR 2026 #6 : That the regular City Council meeting scheduled for Monday, February 2, 2026 be a Roundtable/Working meeting to discuss the results of the 2025 Cambridge Resident Survey.
Policy Order Printout
III. Calendar
Charter Right
1. POR 2025 #172 : Policy Order That the City Manager works with the Government Operations and Civic Unity Committee to hold hearings to discuss new ideas to honor and remember individuals (i.e. communal memorial garden, wall or path), criteria for eligibility, a committee structure with diverse representation of the city residents, and a means for recording and maintaining our current and future memorials. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY MAYOR SIMMONS IN COUNCIL DECEMBER 22, 2025
Policy Order Printout
2. POR 2025 #173 : Restricting Eligibility for On-Street Resident Parking Permits in New Transit-Oriented Developments CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY MAYOR SIMMONS IN COUNCIL DECEMBER 22, 2025
Policy Order Printout
On the Table
3. ORD 2025 # 17 : An Ordinance has been received from Interim City Clerk Paula M. Crane, relative to amend the Zoning Map and Articles 3.000, 17.000, and 19.000 of the Cambridge Zoning Ordinance as follows with the intent of establishing four new zoning districts for the Cambridge Street corridor PASSED TO A SECOND READING AS AMENDED IN COUNCIL DECEMBER 8, 2025 ELIGIBLE TO BE ORDAINED DECEMBER 22, 2025 TABLED IN COUNCIL DECEMBER 22, 2025
b. 20251204_zngamend_cambridge street petition_REVISED_CLEAN
c. 251208.Cambridge Street Zoning Ordinance 2025-17 - First Publication (full text)
IV. Applications and Petitions
1. APP 2026 #1 : An application was received from Brian Lavelle, requesting permission for a curb cut at the premises numbered 18 Thingvalla Avenue; said petition has received approval from Inspectional Services, Traffic, Parking and Transportation, Historical Commission and Public Works. No response has been received from the neighborhood association.
Application and Petitions Printout
a. 18 Thingvalla Ave curb cut (2)
2. APP 2026 #2 : An application was received from Jason Parillo representing Eastern Edge, requesting permission for a two (2) projecting blade signs at the premises numbered 290 Main Street. approval has been received from Inspectional Services, Department of Public Works, Community Development Department and abutters.
Application and Petitions Printout
a. 290 Cambridge Street Scanned Eastern Edge

134. COM 134 #2026 : A communication was received from Tim Russell, regarding Letter regarding Zoning Addmendments_ ORD 2025 #16 &$17.
a. Tim Russell Letter regarding Zoning Addmendments_ ORD 2025 #16 &$17
135. COM 135 #2026 : A communication was received from Nadia Mykolayevych, regarding Please vote NO on the proposed upzoning.
a. A communication was received from Nadia Mykolayevych

a. Heather Hoffman Let the Cambridge Street zoning expire

Shortcut Keys:

Alt + S - Save

Ctrl + S - Save (may not be compatible in all browsers)

Alt + C - Save and Close

Ctrl + Enter - Save and Close

Escape - Closes the form (same as Cancel)

Alt + Up Arrow - Previous Item (Saves if changed)

Alt + Down Arrow - Next Item (Saves if changed)
